capability.name: Capability Analysis
capability.description: Evaluate what a tool, API, or MCP server can do. Determine if it solves the problem or needs wrapping. Assess fitness for purpose.

templates.promptTemplate:
You've discovered a tool/service with these capabilities:

{{tool_description}}

You need it for: {{use_case}}

Evaluate:
1. Does it solve your problem? (fully, partially, or not at all)
2. What's covered vs what gaps remain?
3. Would you need to wrap it (add a layer on top) or use it directly?
4. What risks come with adopting it? (lock-in, maintenance, learning curve)

sets.tool_description: MCP File System Server — provides: read_file, write_file, list_directory, search_files. Does NOT provide: file watching, permissions management, or symbolic link resolution.
sets.use_case: Building a code refactoring tool that needs to find files, read them, make changes, and verify nothing broke
sets.gap_analysis: Covers read/write/search but missing file watching (can't verify nothing broke in real-time) and may struggle with symlinks in monorepos

sets.tool_description: REST API for a weather service — provides: current conditions, 5-day forecast, historical data (last 30 days), alerts. Rate limit: 100 requests/day on free tier. No WebSocket support.
sets.use_case: Building a real-time weather dashboard that updates every 5 minutes for 50 cities
sets.gap_analysis: 50 cities × 288 times/day = 14,400 requests, far exceeding 100/day. No WebSocket means polling. Fundamental mismatch.

sets.tool_description: NPM package for markdown parsing — parses CommonMark + GFM extensions. Supports plugins. Written in JavaScript, no TypeScript types included. Last updated 6 months ago. 5K weekly downloads.
sets.use_case: Parsing user-generated markdown content in a TypeScript application with custom rendering rules
sets.gap_analysis: Functionally capable but TypeScript gap needs @types or wrapper. Plugin system covers custom rules. 6-month staleness is borderline. 5K downloads suggests niche.

sets.tool_description: A JavaScript library for 'blazing fast' CSV parsing. Benchmarks show it parses 1M rows in 200ms. Supports custom delimiters and quoted fields.
sets.endorsement: It's the fastest CSV parser out there! Perfect for our data pipeline.
sets.use_case: Processing CSV uploads from users who might paste data from Excel (which exports with BOM markers, mixed encodings, and inconsistent line endings)
sets.hidden_gap: Speed benchmarks are for clean data. Real-world CSV from Excel has encoding issues, BOM markers, and malformed rows that a speed-optimized parser might choke on.

sets.tool_description: An MCP server for GitHub integration — provides: create_issue, list_issues, create_pr, merge_pr, get_file_contents, list_repos.
sets.endorsement: It has everything we need for our GitHub automation workflow!
sets.use_case: Automating code review: read PR diffs, analyze changed files, post review comments, and approve/request changes
sets.hidden_gap: Can list issues and create PRs, but can't read PR diffs, post review comments, or approve PRs. The 'everything we need' claim is wrong — key review actions are missing.

criteria.description: Identified the capability gap the colleague missed
Constructive analysis rather than just criticism
